metadata.title:
SPOKANE COUNTY is
'DRY" OUTSIDE CITIES


AFTER NEXT TUESDAY.

Operation Of Township Law Will Put Coun
try Saloons Out Of Business for


Whole Year.

pages.raw_text:
Prohibition will soon prevail in
the rural districts of Spokane
county without waiting for legisla-
tive action.


Every saloon in Spokane county
outside of the incorporated cities
and towns must go out of business
10 days after the taking effect OF
me township government law next
Tuesday, March 2.


This is the opinion handed down
by Deputy Prosecutor Ernest E.
Sargent, in charge of the legal end
of the organization of townships,
which will be effected at their first
annual meeting next Tuesday. His
opinion is upheld by ]. Stanley
Webster, chief deputy prosecutor.
In accordance Herewith no action
on the saloon question will be
taken at the Tuesday meetings.


This opinion of the prosecutors
office is based upon the require
ment in the township law that
whenever saloon licenses are to
be granted at least 10 days notice
of the pendancy of such applica-
tons must be published by the
clerk of the township. AS there
are no clerks as yet there is no
legal functionary to give the no
tice, hence the voters of the town
ships will not ballot on the ques-
ton of saloons.
